items.title: Senator Lummis: Ethics, other provisions in crypto Clarity Act to be further discussed
items.summary: Senator Cynthia Lummis, one of the lead negotiators on the bill, told CoinDesk she was "pleased" that the updated bill was ready for release.

items.title: Key Democratic lawmakers say crypto Clarity Act 'falls short' on ethics, other issues
items.summary: Several of the Democrats most likely to vote for the crypto market structure bill said they had issues with the new draft published by Republicans earlier in the day.

items.title: Coinbase Wants to Be Canada's One-Stop Shop for Stocks, Crypto and Prediction Markets
items.summary: The company's "Everything Exchange" is heading north. No launch date set yet, but the Coinbase Canada CEO says phase two for the company is in motion.

items.title: S&P launches blockchain fundamentals index for digital assets
items.summary: The benchmark tracks blockchain networks using protocol revenue, offering an alternative to traditional market capitalization-weighted crypto indexes.

items.title: Tesla holds bitcoin treasury steady, reports $112M impairment loss
items.summary: The electric vehicle maker maintained its 11,509 BTC treasury through the second quarter as bitcoin declined 14%, alongside mixed earnings that beat revenue expectations but missed on profit.

items.title: Crypto PAC pours $1M into Michigan Democratic primary race
items.summary: Donavan McKinney, running against Michigan incumbent Shri Thanedar, said that “the crypto lobby is paying my opponent back for helping Trump“ with the PAC-funded ads.

items.title: Alibaba's New Qwen Image 3 AI Wants to Be Useful, Not Just Pretty
items.summary: Qwen Image 3.0 generates dense newspapers and infographic grids in one shot and renders text down to 10 pixels. The catch: no benchmarks, no open weights.

items.title: Bitcoin Is Bouncing: Here’s the Bull and Bear Case for Its Next Move
items.summary: The good news: Bitcoin is holding onto key support, and rising. The bad news: a "death cross" is still active, and prediction markets are still overwhelmingly bearish.

items.summary: The long-awaited market-structure bill would block officials and their spouses from issuing digital assets and shield non-custodial developers, but the ethics ban expires in 2029 and enforcement rests solely with the DOJ.

items.title: SEC’s Peirce says crypto vaults and onchain lending may fall under securities laws
items.summary: The SEC commissioner said crypto vaults, onchain lending products and other asset management tools may trigger US securities laws depending on how they are structured and operated.

items.title: The digital euro: Surveillance money, or a better alternative to cash?

items.summary: Plus, the BTC ETFs stay green. The Clarity Act hits a wall over who enforces the ethics rules. And Jack Mallers walks away from XXI Capital.

items.title: Zilliqa Ledger app vulnerability lets attackers recover signer’s private keys
items.summary: A security vulnerability in the Zilliqa Ledger app is enabling attackers to reconstruct private keys using publicly available onchain data.

items.summary: Charles Hoskinson says the exploit, which briefly sent NIGHT to an all-time low, underscores why the industry needs to move beyond legacy bridge infrastructure.

items.summary: The breach stemmed from a vulnerability in transaction signing software that enabled the derivation of private keys from blockchain transaction data.

items.title: Jack Dorsey's Block Launches Buzz, a Nostr-Based Slack and GitHub Rival for AI Agents
items.summary: The free, open source workspace runs on Nostr, gives AI agents their own cryptographic identities, and challenges Slack and GitHub.

items.title: Talos brings institutional trading tools to Kalshi prediction markets
items.summary: The integration provides institutional clients with access to Kalshi’s event contracts and crypto perpetuals via Talos’ existing trading infrastructure.

items.summary: Institutional due diligence is shifting toward continuous monitoring, signer controls and incident readiness after operational failures accounted for most crypto losses.

items.title: South Korea crypto volumes shrink as retail investors shift to stocks
items.summary: Trading activity on South Korea’s five major crypto exchanges fell sharply as the KOSPI surged, signaling a shift in retail investors toward equities.

items.title: Foundry asks Bitcoin miners to vote on BIP-110 support

items.summary: SecondFi will wind down after a $2.6 million ADA theft, while users still await recovery tools that were initially expected within weeks of the exploit.

items.title: Galaxy pledges $5M for developers quantum-proofing Bitcoin
